description_summary: Schedules quedan huérfanos al borrar el job asociado: el documento EventSchedule persiste en BD, la API no puede borrarlo, y el webhook no se gatilla porque la asociación job→schedule está rota.

use_cases.title: DELETE schedule-job cascades removal of derived schedule
use_cases.given: Authenticated. Live stream exists. Schedule job created. Derived schedule materialized in GET /api/live-stream/:id/schedule.
use_cases.then: Response 200/204. GET /api/live-stream/:liveId/schedule returns empty list — no orphan EventSchedule remains.
use_cases.notes: Core fix validation. Derived schedule materialization may be async; allow retry loop before asserting empty.

use_cases.title: GET deleted schedule-job returns 404
use_cases.given: Authenticated. Schedule job was created and then deleted.
use_cases.notes: Confirms job is truly gone, not just unlinked.

use_cases.title: DELETE schedule-job twice returns 404 on second attempt
use_cases.given: Authenticated. Schedule job created and already deleted.
use_cases.then: Response 404. body.status = 'ERROR'. No 500 or silent success.
use_cases.notes: Idempotency guard. Previous behavior may have silently failed, leaving schedule in DB.

use_cases.title: GET /schedule list contains no orphan after job delete
use_cases.given: Authenticated. Live stream exists. Job created. Derived schedule visible in GET /schedule. Job then deleted.
use_cases.then: Response 200. Collection does not contain the previously derived schedule ID.
use_cases.notes: Directly validates the orphan-prevention fix. If materialization is async, use retry loop (max 3s).

use_cases.title: GET /schedule/:schedId after orphan state returns 404 or error
use_cases.given: Authenticated. Schedule ID obtained before job deletion. Job then deleted (orphaning the schedule).
use_cases.then: Response 404. body.status = 'ERROR'. API does not return stale/broken schedule data.
use_cases.notes: Validates API-level orphan detection — schedule should not be readable once job is gone.

use_cases.title: Schedule list count stable after cascade delete
use_cases.given: Authenticated. Live stream with 2 schedule jobs, both with derived schedules. One job deleted.
use_cases.then: Both calls return same count. Count = 1 (only schedule for surviving job). No orphan increases count between reads.
use_cases.notes: Extends existing SCHED-RISK-006 stability coverage to post-delete state.

use_cases.title: Webhook not triggered after schedule becomes orphaned
use_cases.given: Authenticated. Schedule job with webhook_url configured exists. Derived schedule materialized.
use_cases.when: DELETE job → schedule becomes orphan (pre-fix behavior) or is cascade-deleted (post-fix)
use_cases.then: POST-fix: no orphan remains → webhook has no trigger surface. API confirms schedule gone.
use_cases.notes: Webhook execution itself is not directly testable via API. Observable proxy: schedule must not exist in GET /schedule after delete. If schedule is gone, webhook cannot fire.

edge_cases.description: Derived schedule materialization is async — GET /schedule may briefly return empty before populating. Use retry loop (3 attempts, 500ms apart) in tests that assert schedule presence.
DELETE job and GET /schedule may race — cascade delete may be async. Tests asserting empty /schedule after delete should allow brief wait.
edge_cases.source: description: 'schedule queda en BD' implies eventual consistency risk

edge_cases.description: Live stream deletion should also clean schedules — if live stream deleted with orphan schedules, those orphans should also be gone.

questions.question: ¿Existe endpoint DELETE /api/live-stream/:id/schedule/:schedId para borrar schedules derivados directamente? No encontrado en tests existentes.
questions.impact: Si existe, agregar UC para testear delete directo de orphan pre-fix.

questions.question: ¿El webhook_url en schedule-job es un campo configurable vía API en el entorno dev? ¿O solo via config interna?
questions.impact: Determina si UC_007 puede configurarse en test o debe marcarse como skip.

questions.question: ¿La corrección es un cascade delete (fix en DELETE job) o un cleanup job/endpoint separado?
questions.impact: Si es cleanup endpoint separado, Agent 2 necesita identificarlo como endpoint adicional a cubrir.
